message:
ci: add failure notification to Dependabot automerge (bd-3a1a)

- Add 'if: failure()' step that creates GitHub issue on automerge failure
  - Includes PR number, title, link, update type, package ecosystem
  - Links to workflow run for debugging
  - Uses 'dependabot' and 'automerge-failure' labels
- Add 'issues: write' permission for issue creation
- Update test script to verify failure notification step exists

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1ak0","title":"Unit Tests: State Management (No Mocks)","description":"Unit Tests: State Management (No Mocks)

Add comprehensive unit tests for state management without mock infrastructure.

## Current Status (Audit)
Existing coverage already present (from prior audit):
- primitives.rs: 17 tests
- lock.rs: 6 tests
- exit_codes.rs: 4 tests
- mod.rs: 3 tests

This bead now focuses on **logging compliance + edge-case expansion** to meet the no-mock standard.

## Target Files (rch/src/state/)
- mod.rs - Core state management module
- lock.rs - State locking mechanisms (15KB)
- exit_codes.rs - Exit code handling
- primitives.rs - State primitives and types (21KB)

## Remaining Test Requirements (No Mocks)
1. **Lock Tests** (lock.rs)
   - Add concurrent contention tests (real threads)
   - Add timeout/backoff coverage
   - Validate deadlock prevention in practice

2. **Exit Code Tests** (exit_codes.rs)
   - Add signal propagation edge cases
   - Validate mapping under error + panic conditions

3. **Primitives Tests** (primitives.rs)
   - Add serialization round-trip with real file I/O
   - Validate invalid inputs and defaults

4. **Integration Tests** (mod.rs)
   - Add state persistence round-trip on disk
   - Add concurrent state access with real locking

## Constraints
- NO MockFileSystem or fake persistence
- Use real temp directories
- Test actual file I/O
- Clean up temp files after tests

## Logging Requirements
- Each test: TEST START/TEST PASS format (per bd-2zsu)
- Log state transitions with timestamps
- Include file contents on persistence failures

## Acceptance Criteria
- Logging compliance across all state tests
- Edge cases covered beyond current 30 tests
- No flaky tests in concurrent scenarios","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T22:59:09.771839816Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T23:54:25.553950733Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T23:54:25.553884499Z","close_reason":"Expanded state management unit tests with TEST START/PASS logging, real file I/O round-trips, and real-thread contention/timeout coverage; verified via cargo test -p rch state","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ak0","depends_on_id":"bd-1aim","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:37Z","created_by":"import"}]}

-{"id":"bd-1cwg","title":"Epic: True End-to-End Integration Tests (No Mocks)","description":"# Epic: True End-to-End Integration Tests (No Mocks)

## Executive Summary

This epic establishes a comprehensive suite of **100% realistic end-to-end integration tests** for RCH (Remote Compilation Helper). Unlike the existing mock-based test suite, these tests will:

- Connect to **real remote workers** via actual SSH
- Execute **real cargo/compiler commands** on those workers
- Transfer **real project files** via rsync
- Retrieve **real build artifacts** back to the local machine
- Validate **output correctness** against local execution

## Merged Scope: Fleet & Worker Lifecycle Tests (formerly bd-guef)

This epic now also includes the fleet and worker lifecycle E2E tests that were previously in a separate epic. This consolidation reduces duplication and provides a single canonical, comprehensive E2E suite.

### Fleet & Worker Lifecycle Coverage

1. **Multi-Worker Scenarios (bd-2s8m)**: Load balancing, priority selection, cached project locality, fleet scaling, heterogeneous workers
2. **Worker Lifecycle**: Registration, health monitoring, removal, updates, disconnects
3. **Test Logging Standardization (bd-2zsu)**: Structured logging across all test files

## Background & Motivation

### Current Test Gap Analysis

The existing RCH test suite provides good coverage for:
- Command classification logic (e2e_hook.rs)
- Daemon lifecycle and API (e2e_daemon.rs)
- Worker connectivity patterns (e2e_worker.rs)
- Fleet operations (e2e_fleet.rs)

However, all tests use **mock infrastructure**:
- `RCH_MOCK_SSH=1` environment variable bypasses real SSH
- `WorkersFixture::mock_local()` creates fake workers
- No actual compilation happens on remote machines
- No real artifact transfer is validated

### Why This Matters

RCH is a **transparent compilation offloading system** for AI coding agents. The agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor) have no awareness that their `cargo build` or `cargo test` commands execute remotely. This transparency requires **absolute correctness** in:

1. **Project synchronization** - Every file the build needs must arrive
2. **Command execution** - Exact same behavior as local execution
3. **Output capture** - Stdout/stderr must be byte-for-byte identical
4. **Exit codes** - Test failures (101), build errors (1) must propagate
5. **Artifact retrieval** - Compiled binaries must work locally

A bug in any of these areas breaks the illusion of local execution and causes agent confusion or failures.

## Test Categories

### Cargo/Rust Compilation (bd-12hi)
- cargo build, test, check, clippy, bench
- All CompilationKind variants

### SSH Infrastructure (bd-255k)
- Connection, authentication, command execution
- Output capture, timeout handling

### Project Sync (bd-1f2v)
- rsync transfer, excludes, incremental sync

### Artifact Transfer (bd-20zz)
- Binary retrieval, correctness verification

### Error Handling & Recovery (bd-23n3)
- Network failures, worker crashes, fail-open behavior
- Circuit breaker state transitions

### C/C++ Compilers (bd-v9pq)
- gcc, g++, clang, make, cmake, ninja

### Output Validation (bd-2ga8)
- Color preservation, ANSI codes, terminal semantics

### Performance & Timing (bd-1nhd)
- Hook classification <1ms, full pipeline <15% overhead

### Multi-Worker Scenarios (bd-2s8m)
- Load balancing, priority selection, heterogeneous workers
- Worker registration, health monitoring, lifecycle

### Test Logging Standardization (bd-2zsu)
- Structured JSON logging across all test files

## Success Criteria

- All CompilationKind variants tested with real workers
- Zero tolerance for output differences from local execution
- Exit codes must match exactly
- Tests pass reliably in CI with real infrastructure
- All tests produce structured, parseable logs
- Worker lifecycle scenarios pass in CI
- Multi-worker load balancing verified
- 100% structured logging compliance","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:10:09.472523589Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T00:27:10.595954929Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1cwg","depends_on_id":"bd-2s8m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:37Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1cwg","depends_on_id":"bd-2zsu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:37Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1cwg","depends_on_id":"bd-3saj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:37Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1djk","title":"Unit Tests for RchConsole Wrapper","description":"## Comprehensive Unit Tests for RchConsole

The RchConsole wrapper is the #1 bottleneck in the dependency graph (blocks 11+ tasks). It must be thoroughly tested.

### Test Categories

#### 1. Context Detection Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_new_detects_context() {
    // This is environment-dependent, so test with explicit context
    let console = RchConsole::with_context(OutputContext::Interactive);
    assert!(console.is_rich());
    assert!(console.is_colored());
    assert!(!console.is_machine());
}

#[test]
fn test_machine_context_properties() {
    let console = RchConsole::with_context(OutputContext::Machine);
    assert!(!console.is_rich());
    assert!(!console.is_colored());
    assert!(console.is_machine());
}

#[test]
fn test_plain_context_properties() {
    let console = RchConsole::with_context(OutputContext::Plain);
    assert!(!console.is_rich());
    assert!(!console.is_colored());
    assert!(!console.is_machine());
}
```

#### 2. Output Suppression Tests
```rust
use std::io::Cursor;

#[test]
fn test_print_rich_suppressed_in_machine_mode() {
    let console = RchConsole::with_context(OutputContext::Machine);
    // This should do nothing, not panic
    console.print_rich("[bold]test[/]");
}

#[test]
fn test_print_plain_suppressed_in_machine_mode() {
    let console = RchConsole::with_context(OutputContext::Machine);
    // This should do nothing
    console.print_plain("test");
}

#[test]
fn test_print_error_always_outputs() {
    let console = RchConsole::with_context(OutputContext::Machine);
    // Errors should still output (to stderr)
    console.print_error("Test Error", "This is a test");
    // Note: Can't easily capture stderr in unit test, but should not panic
}
```

#### 3. Fallback Method Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_print_or_plain_uses_rich_in_interactive() {
    let console = RchConsole::with_context(OutputContext::Interactive);
    // Should use rich path (not panic)
    console.print_or_plain("[bold]rich[/]", "plain");
}

#[test]
fn test_print_or_plain_uses_plain_in_plain_mode() {
    let console = RchConsole::with_context(OutputContext::Plain);
    console.print_or_plain("[bold]rich[/]", "plain");
}

#[test]
fn test_rule_fallback_in_plain_mode() {
    let console = RchConsole::with_context(OutputContext::Plain);
    console.rule(Some("Title"));
    console.rule(None);
}
```

#### 4. JSON Output Tests
```rust
#[derive(serde::Serialize)]
struct TestData {
    key: String,
    value: i32,
}

#[test]
fn test_print_json_serializes_correctly() {
    let console = RchConsole::with_context(OutputContext::Machine);
    let data = TestData { key: "test".to_string(), value: 42 };
    // Should not panic
    let result = console.print_json(&data);
    assert!(result.is_ok());
}
```

#### 5. Width Detection Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_width_returns_reasonable_value() {
    let console = RchConsole::new();
    let width = console.width();
    // Should be at least 40 and at most 500 (reasonable terminal bounds)
    assert!(width >= 40);
    assert!(width <= 500);
}
```

#### 6. Thread Safety Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_global_console_is_thread_safe() {
    use std::thread;
    
    let handles: Vec<_> = (0..10)
        .map(|_| {
            thread::spawn(|| {
                // Access global console from multiple threads
                let _ = CONSOLE.width();
                CONSOLE.print_plain("test");
            })
        })
        .collect();
    
    for handle in handles {
        handle.join().unwrap();
    }
}
```

### Test Coverage Requirements

| Method | Test Coverage |
|--------|--------------|
| new() | Context detection |
| with_context() | All 5 contexts |
| is_rich() | All contexts |
| is_colored() | All contexts |
| is_machine() | All contexts |
| width() | Returns valid value |
| print_rich() | Suppression in non-rich |
| print_plain() | Suppression in machine |
| print_or_plain() | Both paths |
| print_error() | Always outputs |
| print_success/warning/info() | Rich and plain fallbacks |
| print_json() | Serialization |
| rule() | Rich and plain fallbacks |
| CONSOLE static | Thread safety |

 {"id":"bd-39fv","title":"Update dependencies to latest stable versions","description":"# Update Dependencies to Latest Stable Versions

## Purpose
Systematically update all workspace dependencies to their latest stable versions using the library-updater skill.

## Scope
All Cargo.toml files across the workspace:
- `/Cargo.toml` (workspace root)
- `/rch/Cargo.toml` (hook CLI)
- `/rchd/Cargo.toml` (daemon)
- `/rch-wkr/Cargo.toml` (worker agent)
- `/rch-common/Cargo.toml` (shared library)
- `/rch-telemetry/Cargo.toml` (telemetry)

## Process
1. Run `cargo update` to update Cargo.lock
2. Use library-updater skill to check for major version updates
3. Update explicit version pins where safe
4. Run full test suite to verify compatibility
5. Check for deprecation warnings and update usage patterns

## Verification
```bash
cargo check --all-targets
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
cargo test
```

## Notes

 {"id":"bd-3brs","title":"Create RchTheme with color constants and semantic styles","description":"# Create RchTheme with color constants and semantic styles

## Task Description

Create rch-common/src/ui/theme.rs with the RchTheme struct containing all color constants and style generators for consistent visual branding across RCH.

## Background

A unified theme ensures:
1. Visual consistency across all RCH commands
2. Single place to update colors if branding changes
3. Semantic meaning (error = red, success = green)
4. Worker status colors match mental model

## Implementation

### Color Palette

```rust
use rich_rust::prelude::*;

/// RCH brand colors and semantic styles
pub struct RchTheme;

impl RchTheme {
    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
    // BRAND COLORS
    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

    /// Primary brand color - Purple (compilation/build theme)
    pub const PRIMARY: &'static str = "#7C3AED";

    /// Secondary brand color - Cyan (data/transfer theme)
    pub const SECONDARY: &'static str = "#06B6D4";

    /// Accent color - Amber (highlights, attention)
    pub const ACCENT: &'static str = "#F59E0B";

    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
    // SEMANTIC COLORS
    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

    /// Success state - Green
    pub const SUCCESS: &'static str = "#10B981";

    /// Warning state - Amber
    pub const WARNING: &'static str = "#F59E0B";

    /// Error state - Red
    pub const ERROR: &'static str = "#EF4444";

    /// Informational - Blue
    pub const INFO: &'static str = "#3B82F6";

    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
    // WORKER STATUS COLORS
    // These match the WorkerStatus enum from rch-common/types
    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

    /// Worker is healthy and accepting work
    pub const STATUS_HEALTHY: &'static str = "#10B981";     // Green

    /// Worker is degraded (slow, high error rate)
    pub const STATUS_DEGRADED: &'static str = "#F59E0B";    // Amber

    /// Worker is unreachable (connection failed)
    pub const STATUS_UNREACHABLE: &'static str = "#EF4444"; // Red

    /// Worker is draining (no new work)
    pub const STATUS_DRAINING: &'static str = "#8B5CF6";    // Purple

    /// Worker is disabled (admin disabled)
    pub const STATUS_DISABLED: &'static str = "#6B7280";    // Gray

    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
    // TEXT COLORS
    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

    /// Muted text (secondary information)
    pub const MUTED: &'static str = "#9CA3AF";              // Gray-400

    /// Dim text (tertiary information)
    pub const DIM: &'static str = "#6B7280";                // Gray-500

    /// Bright text (emphasis)
    pub const BRIGHT: &'static str = "#F9FAFB";             // Gray-50

    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
    // STYLE GENERATORS
    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

    /// Create style for success messages
    pub fn success() -> Style {
        Style::new().color(Color::parse(Self::SUCCESS).unwrap_or_default())
    }

    /// Create style for error messages (bold for emphasis)
    pub fn error() -> Style {
        Style::new()
            .bold()
            .color(Color::parse(Self::ERROR).unwrap_or_default())
    }

    /// Create style for warning messages
    pub fn warning() -> Style {
        Style::new().color(Color::parse(Self::WARNING).unwrap_or_default())
    }

    /// Create style for informational messages
    pub fn info() -> Style {
        Style::new().color(Color::parse(Self::INFO).unwrap_or_default())
    }

    /// Create style for muted/secondary text
    pub fn muted() -> Style {
        Style::new().color(Color::parse(Self::MUTED).unwrap_or_default())
    }

    /// Create style for dim/tertiary text
    pub fn dim() -> Style {
        Style::new()
            .dim()
            .color(Color::parse(Self::DIM).unwrap_or_default())
    }

    /// Create style for primary brand elements
    pub fn primary() -> Style {
        Style::new().color(Color::parse(Self::PRIMARY).unwrap_or_default())
    }

    /// Create style for secondary brand elements
    pub fn secondary() -> Style {
        Style::new().color(Color::parse(Self::SECONDARY).unwrap_or_default())
    }

    /// Create style for worker status based on WorkerStatus enum
    pub fn worker_status(status: &str) -> Style {
        let color = match status.to_lowercase().as_str() {
            "healthy" => Self::STATUS_HEALTHY,
            "degraded" => Self::STATUS_DEGRADED,
            "unreachable" => Self::STATUS_UNREACHABLE,
            "draining" => Self::STATUS_DRAINING,
            "disabled" => Self::STATUS_DISABLED,
            _ => Self::MUTED,
        };
        Style::new().color(Color::parse(color).unwrap_or_default())
    }

    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
    // COMPOSITE STYLES
    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

    /// Style for table headers
    pub fn table_header() -> Style {
        Style::new()
            .bold()
            .color(Color::parse(Self::BRIGHT).unwrap_or_default())
    }

    /// Style for table borders
    pub fn table_border() -> Style {
        Style::new().color(Color::parse(Self::PRIMARY).unwrap_or_default())
    }

    /// Style for panel titles
    pub fn panel_title() -> Style {
        Style::new()
            .bold()
            .color(Color::parse(Self::SECONDARY).unwrap_or_default())
    }

    /// Style for command/code text
    pub fn code() -> Style {
        Style::new()
            .color(Color::parse(Self::SECONDARY).unwrap_or_default())
    }

    /// Style for paths/filenames
    pub fn path() -> Style {
        Style::new()
            .italic()
            .color(Color::parse(Self::INFO).unwrap_or_default())
    }

    /// Style for numbers/metrics
    pub fn number() -> Style {
        Style::new().color(Color::parse(Self::ACCENT).unwrap_or_default())
    }
}
```

### Integration with WorkerStatus Type

```rust

    pub fn for_worker_status(status: WorkerStatus) -> Style {
        let color = match status {
            WorkerStatus::Healthy => Self::STATUS_HEALTHY,
            WorkerStatus::Degraded => Self::STATUS_DEGRADED,
            WorkerStatus::Unreachable => Self::STATUS_UNREACHABLE,

            WorkerStatus::Disabled => Self::STATUS_DISABLED,
        };
```

## Color Choice Rationale

### Brand Colors
- **Purple (#7C3AED)**: Compilation/build - sophisticated, technical
- **Cyan (#06B6D4)**: Data transfer - clean, digital
- **Amber (#F59E0B)**: Attention - warm, visible

### Semantic Colors
- **Green (#10B981)**: Success - universal meaning
- **Amber (#F59E0B)**: Warning - caution without alarm
- **Red (#EF4444)**: Error - immediate attention
- **Blue (#3B82F6)**: Info - calm, informational

### Worker Status
- **Green**: Healthy - good to go
- **Amber**: Degraded - working but suboptimal
- **Red**: Unreachable - needs attention
- **Purple**: Draining - intentional state
- **Gray**: Disabled - inactive

```rust
    }

        let _ = RchTheme::success();
        let _ = RchTheme::error();
        let _ = RchTheme::warning();
        let _ = RchTheme::info();
        let _ = RchTheme::worker_status("healthy");
        let _ = RchTheme::worker_status("unknown"); // Should not panic
    }
}
```

 {"id":"bd-3c8d","title":"E2E Test Script: Self-Healing Validation","description":"# E2E Test Script: Self-Healing Validation

## Overview
A comprehensive end-to-end test script that validates the entire self-healing system works correctly in real-world scenarios. This script should be runnable by developers and CI to ensure the self-healing features work as designed.

## Test Script Location
`tests/e2e/test_self_healing.sh`

## Script Structure

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# =============================================================================
# E2E Test: RCH Self-Healing System
# =============================================================================
#
# This script validates the mutually reinforcing self-healing behavior:
# 1. Hook auto-starts daemon when unavailable
# 2. Daemon auto-installs hooks on startup
# 3. Doctor --fix repairs both
#
# Prerequisites:
# - rch and rchd binaries in PATH
# - Write access to ~/.claude/ and /tmp/
# - No production daemon running (test uses isolated config)
#
# Usage:
#   ./tests/e2e/test_self_healing.sh [--verbose]
#

# =============================================================================
# Configuration
# =============================================================================

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
TEST_HOME=$(mktemp -d)
TEST_CONFIG_DIR="${TEST_HOME}/.config/rch"
TEST_CLAUDE_DIR="${TEST_HOME}/.claude"
TEST_SOCKET="/tmp/rch-test-$$.sock"
LOG_FILE="${TEST_HOME}/test.log"

VERBOSE="${1:-}"
PASSED=0
FAILED=0
TOTAL=0

# Colors
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
BLUE='\033[0;34m'
NC='\033[0m' # No Color

# =============================================================================
# Logging Functions
# =============================================================================

log() {
    local level=$1
    shift
    local timestamp=$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S.%3N')
    echo -e "[$timestamp] [$level] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
}

log_info()  { log "INFO " "$*"; }
log_pass()  { log "${GREEN}PASS${NC} " "$*"; }
log_fail()  { log "${RED}FAIL${NC} " "$*"; }
log_test()  { log "${BLUE}TEST${NC} " "$*"; }
log_detail() { [[ "$VERBOSE" == "--verbose" ]] && log "DEBUG" "$*" || true; }

# =============================================================================
# Test Utilities
# =============================================================================

setup_test_env() {
    log_info "Setting up isolated test environment..."
    log_detail "TEST_HOME=$TEST_HOME"
    
    mkdir -p "$TEST_CONFIG_DIR"
    mkdir -p "$TEST_CLAUDE_DIR"
    
    # Create minimal config pointing to test socket
    cat > "${TEST_CONFIG_DIR}/config.toml" <<EOF
socket_path = "${TEST_SOCKET}"

[self_healing]
hook_starts_daemon = true
daemon_installs_hooks = true
daemon_start_timeout = 5
auto_start_cooldown = 5
EOF
    
    export HOME="$TEST_HOME"
    export RCH_CONFIG="${TEST_CONFIG_DIR}/config.toml"
    export RCH_SOCKET_PATH="$TEST_SOCKET"
    
    log_info "Test environment ready"
}

cleanup() {
    log_info "Cleaning up test environment..."
    
    # Stop any test daemon
    if [[ -S "$TEST_SOCKET" ]]; then
        log_detail "Stopping test daemon..."
        rch daemon stop 2>/dev/null || true
    fi
    
    # Kill any stray rchd processes from this test
    pkill -f "rchd.*${TEST_SOCKET}" 2>/dev/null || true
    
    # Remove test directory
    if [[ -d "$TEST_HOME" && "$TEST_HOME" == /tmp/* ]]; then
        rm -rf "$TEST_HOME"
    fi
    
    log_info "Cleanup complete"
}

trap cleanup EXIT

assert_eq() {
    local expected=$1
    local actual=$2
    local msg=$3
    
    if [[ "$expected" == "$actual" ]]; then
        return 0
    else
        log_fail "Assertion failed: $msg"
        log_detail "  Expected: $expected"
        log_detail "  Actual:   $actual"
        return 1
    fi
}

assert_file_exists() {
    local file=$1
    local msg=$2
    
    if [[ -f "$file" ]]; then
        return 0
    else
        log_fail "File not found: $file ($msg)"
        return 1
    fi
}

assert_socket_exists() {
    local socket=$1
    local msg=$2
    
    if [[ -S "$socket" ]]; then
        return 0
    else
        log_fail "Socket not found: $socket ($msg)"
        return 1
    fi
}

assert_contains() {
    local haystack=$1
    local needle=$2
    local msg=$3
    
    if [[ "$haystack" == *"$needle"* ]]; then
        return 0
    else
        log_fail "String not found: '$needle' ($msg)"
        log_detail "  In: $haystack"
        return 1
    fi
}

run_test() {
    local name=$1
    shift
    local test_fn=$1
    
    TOTAL=$((TOTAL + 1))
    log_test "Running: $name"
    
    if $test_fn; then
        PASSED=$((PASSED + 1))
        log_pass "$name"
        return 0
    else
        FAILED=$((FAILED + 1))
        log_fail "$name"
        return 1
    fi
}

# =============================================================================
# Test Cases
# =============================================================================

test_hook_auto_starts_daemon() {
    log_detail "Ensuring daemon is stopped..."
    rch daemon stop 2>/dev/null || true
    rm -f "$TEST_SOCKET"
    sleep 1
    
    # Verify daemon not running
    if [[ -S "$TEST_SOCKET" ]]; then
        log_fail "Precondition failed: daemon still running"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_detail "Creating test project..."
    local test_project="${TEST_HOME}/test_project"
    mkdir -p "$test_project"
    cat > "${test_project}/Cargo.toml" <<EOF
[package]
name = "test_project"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"
EOF
    mkdir -p "${test_project}/src"
    echo 'fn main() { println!("Hello"); }' > "${test_project}/src/main.rs"
    
    log_detail "Invoking hook (should auto-start daemon)..."
    local hook_input='{"event":"PreToolUse","tool_name":"Bash","tool_input":{"command":"cargo build --release"},"session_id":"test","session_cwd":"'"$test_project"'"}'
    
    local output
    output=$(echo "$hook_input" | timeout 30 rch 2>&1) || true
    
    log_detail "Hook output: $output"
    
    # Check if daemon was auto-started
    sleep 2
    if ! [[ -S "$TEST_SOCKET" ]]; then
        log_fail "Daemon was not auto-started"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_detail "Daemon auto-started successfully"
    return 0
}

test_daemon_auto_installs_hook() {
    log_detail "Removing existing hook..."
    rm -f "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json"
    
    log_detail "Stopping daemon..."
    rch daemon stop 2>/dev/null || true
    rm -f "$TEST_SOCKET"
    sleep 1
    
    log_detail "Starting daemon (should install hook)..."
    rch daemon start
    sleep 2
    
    # Check if hook was installed
    if ! assert_file_exists "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json" "Hook settings file should exist"; then
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Verify hook content
    local settings
    settings=$(cat "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json")
    
    if ! assert_contains "$settings" '"command": "rch"' "Settings should contain rch hook"; then
        log_detail "Settings content: $settings"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_detail "Hook auto-installed successfully"
    return 0
}

test_daemon_preserves_existing_hooks() {
    log_detail "Creating settings with existing DCG hook..."
    mkdir -p "$TEST_CLAUDE_DIR"
    cat > "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json" <<EOF
{
  "hooks": {
    "PreToolUse": [
      {
        "command": "dcg",
        "description": "Destructive Command Guard"
      }
    ]
  }
}
EOF
    
    log_detail "Restarting daemon..."
    rch daemon stop 2>/dev/null || true
    rm -f "$TEST_SOCKET"
    sleep 1
    rch daemon start
    sleep 2
    
    # Verify both hooks present
    local settings
    settings=$(cat "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json")
    
    if ! assert_contains "$settings" '"command": "dcg"' "DCG hook should be preserved"; then
        return 1
    fi
    
    if ! assert_contains "$settings" '"command": "rch"' "RCH hook should be added"; then
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_detail "Existing hooks preserved successfully"
    return 0
}

test_doctor_fix_installs_hook() {
    log_detail "Removing hook..."
    rm -f "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json"
    
    log_detail "Running doctor --fix..."
    local output
    output=$(rch doctor --fix 2>&1)
    
    log_detail "Doctor output: $output"
    
    if ! assert_file_exists "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json" "Doctor should install hook"; then
        return 1
    fi
    
    if ! assert_contains "$output" "Fixed" "Doctor should report fix"; then
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_detail "Doctor --fix installed hook successfully"
    return 0
}

test_doctor_fix_starts_daemon() {
    log_detail "Stopping daemon..."
    rch daemon stop 2>/dev/null || true
    rm -f "$TEST_SOCKET"
    sleep 1
    
    log_detail "Running doctor --fix..."
    local output
    output=$(rch doctor --fix 2>&1)
    
    log_detail "Doctor output: $output"
    
    sleep 2
    if ! assert_socket_exists "$TEST_SOCKET" "Doctor should start daemon"; then
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_detail "Doctor --fix started daemon successfully"
    return 0
}

test_doctor_dry_run_no_changes() {
    log_detail "Stopping daemon and removing hook..."
    rch daemon stop 2>/dev/null || true
    rm -f "$TEST_SOCKET"
    rm -f "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json"
    sleep 1
    
    log_detail "Running doctor --fix --dry-run..."
    local output
    output=$(rch doctor --fix --dry-run 2>&1)
    
    log_detail "Doctor output: $output"
    
    # Verify no changes made
    if [[ -f "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json" ]]; then
        log_fail "Dry run should not create settings file"
        return 1
    fi
    
    if [[ -S "$TEST_SOCKET" ]]; then
        log_fail "Dry run should not start daemon"
        return 1
    fi
    
    if ! assert_contains "$output" "Would" "Dry run should say 'Would'"; then
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_detail "Dry run made no changes as expected"
    return 0
}

test_auto_start_cooldown() {
    log_detail "This test validates cooldown prevents restart spam"
    
    # Start and immediately stop daemon
    rch daemon start
    sleep 1
    rch daemon stop
    rm -f "$TEST_SOCKET"
    
    # Record time
    local start_time=$(date +%s)
    
    # Try to trigger auto-start via hook
    local test_project="${TEST_HOME}/test_project2"
    mkdir -p "$test_project/src"
    cat > "${test_project}/Cargo.toml" <<EOF
[package]
name = "test_project2"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"
EOF
    echo 'fn main() {}' > "${test_project}/src/main.rs"
    
    local hook_input='{"event":"PreToolUse","tool_name":"Bash","tool_input":{"command":"cargo build"},"session_id":"test","session_cwd":"'"$test_project"'"}'
    
    log_detail "First hook invocation..."
    echo "$hook_input" | timeout 10 rch 2>&1 || true
    
    # Check if cooldown file was created
    local cooldown_file="/tmp/rch-autostart-cooldown"
    if [[ -f "$cooldown_file" ]]; then
        log_detail "Cooldown file created"
    fi
    
    log_detail "Second hook invocation (should skip due to cooldown)..."
    local output
    output=$(echo "$hook_input" | timeout 5 rch 2>&1) || true
    
    # The second invocation should be fast (not waiting for daemon)
    # because cooldown prevents restart attempt
    local end_time=$(date +%s)
    local elapsed=$((end_time - start_time))
    
    log_detail "Elapsed time: ${elapsed}s"
    
    # If cooldown works, second invocation should be quick
    # (This is a heuristic test - timing-based)
    return 0
}

test_config_disables_self_healing() {
    log_detail "Creating config with self-healing disabled..."
    cat > "${TEST_CONFIG_DIR}/config.toml" <<EOF
socket_path = "${TEST_SOCKET}"

[self_healing]
hook_starts_daemon = false
daemon_installs_hooks = false
EOF
    
    # Stop daemon and remove hook
    rch daemon stop 2>/dev/null || true
    rm -f "$TEST_SOCKET"
    rm -f "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json"
    sleep 1
    
    log_detail "Starting daemon (should NOT install hook)..."
    rch daemon start
    sleep 2
    
    # Hook should NOT be installed (disabled in config)
    if [[ -f "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json" ]]; then
        local settings
        settings=$(cat "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json")
        if [[ "$settings" == *'"command": "rch"'* ]]; then
            log_fail "Hook was installed despite being disabled in config"
            return 1
        fi
    fi
    
    log_detail "Self-healing correctly disabled by config"
    
    # Restore default config
    cat > "${TEST_CONFIG_DIR}/config.toml" <<EOF
socket_path = "${TEST_SOCKET}"

[self_healing]
hook_starts_daemon = true
daemon_installs_hooks = true
EOF
    
    return 0
}

test_full_self_healing_cycle() {
    log_detail "=== Full Self-Healing Cycle Test ==="
    
    # Start from completely clean state
    log_detail "Step 1: Clean state"
    rch daemon stop 2>/dev/null || true
    rm -f "$TEST_SOCKET"
    rm -f "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json"
    rm -rf "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}"
    sleep 1
    
    # Verify nothing exists
    if [[ -S "$TEST_SOCKET" ]] || [[ -f "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json" ]]; then
        log_fail "Clean state not achieved"
        return 1
    fi
    log_detail "  ✓ Clean state verified"
    
    # Run doctor --fix (should fix everything)
    log_detail "Step 2: Run doctor --fix"
    local output
    output=$(rch doctor --fix 2>&1)
    log_detail "Doctor output: $output"
    sleep 2
    
    # Verify hook installed
    if ! [[ -f "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json" ]]; then
        log_fail "Hook not installed by doctor --fix"
        return 1
    fi
    log_detail "  ✓ Hook installed"
    
    # Verify daemon running
    if ! [[ -S "$TEST_SOCKET" ]]; then
        log_fail "Daemon not started by doctor --fix"
        return 1
    fi
    log_detail "  ✓ Daemon running"
    
    # Simulate daemon crash
    log_detail "Step 3: Simulate daemon crash"
    pkill -f "rchd.*${TEST_SOCKET}" 2>/dev/null || true
    rm -f "$TEST_SOCKET"
    sleep 1
    
    if [[ -S "$TEST_SOCKET" ]]; then
        log_fail "Daemon still running after simulated crash"
        return 1
    fi
    log_detail "  ✓ Daemon crashed (simulated)"
    
    # Trigger build (hook should auto-restart daemon)
    log_detail "Step 4: Trigger build (should auto-restart daemon)"
    local test_project="${TEST_HOME}/test_project_cycle"
    mkdir -p "$test_project/src"
    cat > "${test_project}/Cargo.toml" <<EOF
[package]
name = "cycle_test"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"
EOF
    echo 'fn main() { println!("cycle test"); }' > "${test_project}/src/main.rs"
    
    local hook_input='{"event":"PreToolUse","tool_name":"Bash","tool_input":{"command":"cargo build --release"},"session_id":"cycle-test","session_cwd":"'"$test_project"'"}'
    
    output=$(echo "$hook_input" | timeout 30 rch 2>&1) || true
    log_detail "Hook output: $output"
    sleep 2
    
    # Verify daemon auto-restarted
    if ! [[ -S "$TEST_SOCKET" ]]; then
        log_fail "Daemon not auto-restarted by hook"
        return 1
    fi
    log_detail "  ✓ Daemon auto-restarted"
    
    log_detail "=== Full cycle complete ==="
    return 0
}

    run_test "Daemon auto-installs hook" test_daemon_auto_installs_hook
    run_test "Daemon preserves existing hooks" test_daemon_preserves_existing_hooks
    run_test "Doctor --fix installs hook" test_doctor_fix_installs_hook
    run_test "Doctor --fix starts daemon" test_doctor_fix_starts_daemon
    run_test "Doctor --dry-run makes no changes" test_doctor_dry_run_no_changes
    run_test "Auto-start cooldown works" test_auto_start_cooldown
    run_test "Config disables self-healing" test_config_disables_self_healing

    echo "  Test Results"
```

Test that rch correctly classifies and offloads C/C++ compilation commands (gcc, g++, clang, clang++, make, cmake, ninja).

## MANDATORY Logging Requirements

All C/C++ tests MUST use structured JSON logging:

### C/C++ Specific Log Events

```json
// Compiler detection

// Remote execution
```

### Metrics to Log

1. **Compiler info**: name, version, path
2. **Classification**: tier, kind, decision rationale
3. **Execution**: command, exit code, duration, worker
4. **Artifacts**: paths, sizes, executable status
5. **Build systems**: targets built, dependencies resolved

## Why This Matters

- Many projects mix Rust with C/C++ dependencies
- Build systems like make/cmake invoke compilers that could benefit from offloading
- C/C++ classification is more complex than Rust (many compiler flags, toolchains)
- Getting this wrong could break builds or miss offloading opportunities

### Advanced Tests

8. **Cross-compilation** (if workers have cross-compilers)

- [ ] make/cmake/ninja builds complete successfully on workers

- [ ] Compiler errors propagated correctly with exit codes

- [ ] Compiler version and classification logged for every test

```bash
# Configure a worker with invalid host
export RCH_WORKERS='[{"host":"nonexistent.local","port":22,"user":"test"}]'

STDERR_FILE=$(mktemp)
"$RCH" workers probe 2>"$STDERR_FILE" || true

# Verify error contains expected elements
check_stderr_contains "$STDERR_FILE" "RCH-E0" "Error code missing"
check_stderr_contains "$STDERR_FILE" "nonexistent.local" "Host not shown"
check_stderr_contains "$STDERR_FILE" -i "check\|verify\|ensure" "No remediation"

# Verify no ANSI codes if NO_COLOR is set
NO_COLOR=1 "$RCH" workers probe 2>"$STDERR_FILE" || true
if grep -qP '\x1b\[' "$STDERR_FILE"; then
    fail "ANSI codes present despite NO_COLOR"
fi

log_json "verify" "SSH error display correct"

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TEST 2: Config Parse Error
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log_json "test" "Config parse error display"

INVALID_CONFIG=$(mktemp --suffix=.toml)
cat > "$INVALID_CONFIG" << 'EOF'
[[workers]]
host = "test.local
# Missing closing quote - parse error
EOF

export RCH_CONFIG="$INVALID_CONFIG"
"$RCH" status 2>"$STDERR_FILE" || true

# Verify error shows file and line
check_stderr_contains "$STDERR_FILE" ".toml" "Config file not shown"
check_stderr_contains "$STDERR_FILE" "line\|Line\|:" "Line number missing"

rm "$INVALID_CONFIG"
log_json "verify" "Config error display correct"

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TEST 3: Build Failure Error
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log_json "test" "Build failure error display"

# Create project with intentional compile error
TEST_PROJECT=$(mktemp -d)
cd "$TEST_PROJECT"
cargo init --name test_fail
cat > src/main.rs << 'EOF'
fn main() {
    let x: i32 = "not a number"; // Type error
}
EOF

# Run through RCH hook
HOOK_INPUT='{"tool":"Bash","input":{"command":"cargo build"}}' 
echo "$HOOK_INPUT" | "$RCH" hook --stdin 2>"$STDERR_FILE" || true

# Compiler errors should be preserved and visible
check_stderr_contains "$STDERR_FILE" "E0308\|mismatched" "Compiler error code missing"

cd -
rm -rf "$TEST_PROJECT"
log_json "verify" "Build error display correct"

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TEST 4: Error JSON Mode
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log_json "test" "Error JSON output mode"

export RCH_WORKERS='[{"host":"nonexistent.local"}]'
"$RCH" --json workers probe 2>"$STDERR_FILE" >"$STDOUT_FILE" || true

# JSON mode should output structured error to stdout
if [[ -s "$STDOUT_FILE" ]]; then
    if ! jq -e '.error' "$STDOUT_FILE" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        fail "JSON error output not valid"
    fi
    
    # Should have error code
    jq -e '.error.code' "$STDOUT_FILE" >/dev/null || warn "No error code in JSON"
fi

log_json "verify" "Error JSON mode correct"

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TEST 5: Error Severity Differentiation
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log_json "test" "Error severity visual differentiation"

# Warning (non-fatal)
"$RCH" workers list --include-offline 2>"$STDERR_FILE" || true
WARNINGS=$(cat "$STDERR_FILE")

# Error (fatal) 
export RCH_WORKERS='invalid json'
"$RCH" status 2>"$STDERR_FILE" || true
ERRORS=$(cat "$STDERR_FILE")

# They should look different (different styling/icons)
# At minimum, both should render without crash

log_json "verify" "Error severities differentiated"

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TEST 6: Error to stderr, Never stdout
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log_json "test" "Errors go to stderr exclusively"

export RCH_WORKERS='[{"host":"nonexistent.local"}]'

STDOUT_FILE=$(mktemp)
STDERR_FILE=$(mktemp)
"$RCH" workers probe >"$STDOUT_FILE" 2>"$STDERR_FILE" || true

# stdout should be empty or valid JSON (for machine consumption)
if [[ -s "$STDOUT_FILE" ]]; then
    # If non-empty, must be valid JSON
    if ! jq -e . "$STDOUT_FILE" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        fail "Non-JSON content in stdout during error"
    fi
fi

# stderr should have the error message
if [[ ! -s "$STDERR_FILE" ]]; then
    fail "No error message in stderr"
fi

log_json "verify" "Error streams correct"

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SUMMARY
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log_json "summary" "All error display tests passed"
echo ""
echo "═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
echo "ALL ERROR DISPLAY TESTS PASSED"
echo "═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
```

 {"id":"bd-x1ek","title":"Idea: Retry/backoff for rsync + SSH exec","description":"## Background
Transient network failures and SSH hiccups cause unnecessary fallback to local builds. Simple retry with backoff improves reliability without risking long stalls.

## Goals
- Retry rsync upload/download and SSH command execution on transient errors.
- Limit retries and total time to preserve responsiveness.
- Emit clear logs with retry counts and reasons.

## Design / Approach
- Introduce retry policy in transfer pipeline (max_attempts, base_delay, jitter).
- Classify retryable errors (timeout, connection reset) vs non-retryable (auth, host key).
- Respect overall timeouts and fail-open if exceeded.

## Tasks / Subtasks
- Add retry policy to config with safe defaults.
- Implement retry wrapper for rsync + SSH exec.
- Update mock transport to simulate retryable failures.

## Tests
- Unit: retryable error classification.
- Integration: mock transport fails once then succeeds.
- E2E: timeouts do not exceed configured caps.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Transient failures recover without manual retry.
- Non-retryable errors still fail fast.

## Logging & E2E
- E2E script: scripts/e2e_bd-x1ek.sh (one script per bead unless intentionally combined).

- Phases to log (as applicable): classify, select, sync_up, exec, sync_down, cleanup, summary.
- Include a final summary block with pass/fail counts and total elapsed time.

## Edge Cases & UX
- Only retry when command definitely did NOT start (connect/transport errors).
- Exponential backoff with jitter and total time cap.
- Non-retryable errors (auth/host key) fail fast.

- Mock auth failure -> no retry.
- Logs include attempt count + total elapsed time.

- rch/src/transfer.rs: retry policy caps total time.

- Verify non-retryable errors fail fast.
